claim: Agent failures are fundamentally different from traditional software failures because they are non-deterministic, unbounded in input/output space, and can arbitrarily affect other systems
actionable: When instrumenting agent monitoring, plan for detection of semantic failures (user frustration, task failure) rather than relying solely on error codes.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Evals are insufficient for production agent monitoring because agents can access combinatorially expanding tool sets and memory sources, creating edge cases no golden dataset covers
actionable: Move beyond eval-only validation; instrument production agents with explicit signals (error rate, latency, cost) and implicit signals (classifiers for refusal, frustration, task failure).
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Regex patterns for signal detection (e.g., 'WTF', 'this sucks', 'horrible') aggregate into robust failure signals even with noise, as evidenced by Claude Code's internal monitoring
actionable: Build regex signal detectors for common failure patterns (frustration, suspicion, task abandonment) and track their rates over time; spike detection catches regressions.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Binary classifiers trained on semantic signals (refusal, task failure, user frustration) are more valuable for regression detection than LLM-as-judge scoring systems
actionable: Train lightweight binary classifiers offline rather than running Claude on every output; deploy them in production to label events for alerting and experiment validation.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Running LLMs on every agent output to score quality doubles AI spend and becomes cost-prohibitive at scale; training small custom models is necessary instead
actionable: At >100k events/day, train specialized classifiers rather than prompting LLMs; the cost/latency trade-off overwhelmingly favors custom models.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: A-B experiments on agent behavior require only a few hundred events to detect patterns; signal spikes pointing to specific tools or prompts often provide actionable insights before statistical significance
actionable: When shipping agent changes, run experiments with as few as 200-500 events; validate with signal trends (user frustration, tool errors) rather than waiting for p-values.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Self-diagnostics—prompting agents to report their own failures, misconduct, and capability gaps—works when framed as feedback to creators rather than confession
actionable: Add a simple tool to your agent's system prompt asking it to 'report noteworthy behavior to your creators' before final answers; label it neutrally, not punitively.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Models resist self-incrimination and self-correction unless the tool/prompt is framed positively; naming a tool 'report' rather than 'unsafe_behavior' dramatically increases trigger rate
actionable: When designing self-diagnostic prompts, test neutral/positive framings before settling on punitive ones; models will admit fault to their creators but not to judges.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Agents can detect tool failures, capability gaps, user frustration, and self-correction/workarounds by inspecting their own reasoning traces
actionable: Design agents to introspect on trace patterns: repeated tool failures, unmet user requests, and shortcuts taken; surface these as signals for monitoring and debugging.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Capability gaps detected automatically (e.g., 'user wanted to set alerts but agent has no alert tool') serve as pseudo-feature requests when agents can report them
actionable: Log agent-detected capability gaps as structured feedback; prioritize the highest-frequency gaps as feature candidates.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Multi-turn agent observability is where signal collection becomes most valuable, as single-turn agents lose many implicit signal opportunities
actionable: Prioritize observability tooling for multi-turn agents and conversational workflows; single-turn systems can rely more on explicit signals.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability

claim: Observability is becoming more important than testing/evals for catching the long tail of production agent failures as they scale
actionable: Allocate engineering effort to production monitoring and alerting for agents earlier in the development cycle, not as an afterthought.
source_title: Everything You Need To Know About Agent Observability
